name: align_result_not_aligned
reason: The whole purpose of align_to_usize is to produce an aligned address; if the result is not aligned the function is broken
output_tail:
------------------------------------------------- at the end of the function body

1391 |         !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
     |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition

name: aligned_successor_also_aligned
reason: If addr is aligned to size>1 then addr+1 cannot also be aligned, so this being provable would mean aligned is trivially true
output_tail:
(addr: nat, size: nat)

...
1392 |         aligned(addr + 1, size),
     |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition

name: align_drops_too_much
reason: The rounding loss must be strictly less than b; losing b or more means we skipped an entire alignment boundary
output_tail:
ch(a: usize, b: usize)

...
1392 |         a - align_to_usize(a, b) >= b,
     |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition
